title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
role_summary: PM for YouTube Create — a consumer-facing creator tool product within YouTube, focused on enabling video creation and editing experiences for creators.
why_good_fit: Jay's creator economy experience is directly transferable: he scaled Resso from alpha to India's largest music streaming app, worked on TikTok artist activation (Ghost Music Tab), led Shoppertainment strategy at Meta converging entertainment and commerce for Indian creators, and contributed to content relevance and creator growth initiatives at Meta. His deep understanding of regional/long-tail creators and Indian GenZ content consumption (GenZ Product Council member) is a massive advantage for a YouTube creation tool targeting India-based creators. 5 years PM experience required matches Jay's ~7 years perfectly.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story — zero-to-one creator product, understanding what Tier 2/3 Indian creators need vs. what global products assume. Pitch the insight that Indian creators operate differently (voice-led, regional language, mobile-first) and how YouTube Create can win by localising the creation experience. Reference the 10%/90% content insight from Resso to show data-driven product thinking for creator tools. Demonstrate knowledge of YouTube Shorts creation workflow gaps vs. competitors.
job_description: You'll own the product roadmap for YouTube Create, a creation tool that helps YouTubers produce and edit content. Day-to-day involves defining features, working with engineering and design, running user research with creators, and shipping improvements that drive creator adoption and content quality. Success means more creators using YouTube Create to produce content that drives engagement on the platform.

title: Product Manager, YouTube Live Living Room
role_summary: PM for YouTube's live streaming experience on TV/living room devices — a consumer video product at the intersection of live content and the connected TV surface.
why_good_fit: Jay has direct experience in live streaming and entertainment products: Resso (music streaming at scale), Shoppertainment (live commerce convergence), and deep understanding of Indian consumer content consumption patterns. His GenZ Product Council membership gives him insight into how younger audiences consume live content. The 3-year experience minimum is well within Jay's range, and the consumer video product focus aligns perfectly with his ByteDance + Meta background. His field work understanding of how Indian families consume content on shared devices is a unique edge.
how_to_win: Frame the living room as the next frontier for Indian content consumption — reference field observations of how Indian households use smart TVs differently (shared viewing, regional content, live events like cricket). Pitch the Shoppertainment angle: live commerce on living room screens is an untapped opportunity. Use the Resso story to show you understand how to build engagement loops for entertainment products. Highlight your competitive intelligence work (TikTok re-entry) to show you can position YouTube Live against competitors.
job_description: You'll own the YouTube Live experience on TV and living room devices — defining what live streaming looks like on the big screen. Day-to-day involves working with engineering, UX, and content teams to improve live content discovery, viewer engagement, and creator tools for the TV surface. Success is measured by live viewership growth and engagement on connected TV devices.

title: Product Manager, Google Pay
role_summary: PM for Google Pay — India's leading consumer payments product, serving hundreds of millions of Indian users across UPI, merchant payments, and financial services.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is fundamentally an India market growth product — exactly Jay's superpower. His SMB work at Meta (bakery insight → 10M businesses, 2.3% revenue uplift) directly maps to Google Pay's merchant/SMB payment ecosystem. His understanding of informal Indian businesses, voice-led operations, and Tier 2/3 user behaviour is critical for GPay's expansion. The Resso thesis of targeting users ignored by global products mirrors GPay's need to serve underbanked/underserved Indian users. 5 years PM experience required is a perfect match.
how_to_win: Lead with the Dahod bakery story — reframe it as understanding how Indian SMBs actually transact (cash + UPI + WhatsApp, not structured digital payments). Pitch that you understand the gap between how global fintech products assume Indian users pay vs. how they actually pay. Reference your SMB enforcement work to show you can identify systemic product issues affecting millions of small businesses. Demonstrate knowledge of GPay's competitive landscape (PhonePe, Paytm) using your competitive intelligence methodology.
job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Pay India — potentially merchant payments, P2P, financial services, or growth. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy, running experiments, working with engineering and design, and driving adoption metrics. Success means growing Google Pay's user base, transaction volume, or merchant ecosystem in India.

title: Product Manager, eWallets
role_summary: PM for Google's eWallet products — digital wallet experiences likely spanning Google Pay and broader payments infrastructure for consumer and merchant use cases.
why_good_fit: Similar to Google Pay, this role benefits from Jay's deep India market intelligence around digital payments behaviour. His SMB work at Meta (understanding how informal businesses handle money) and his Tier 2/3 user expertise from Resso are directly relevant. Jay's understanding of Indian digital literacy patterns — voice-led, informal, mobile-first — is critical for eWallet product design. The 5-year experience requirement matches perfectly.
how_to_win: Position yourself as someone who understands the gap between digital wallet product assumptions and actual Indian user behaviour. Use the bakery story to illustrate how Indian SMBs manage money informally. Reference your field work methodology — 20+ user interviews, on-ground observation — as the approach you'd bring to understanding eWallet usage patterns. Show awareness of India's UPI ecosystem and how eWallets need to differentiate.
job_description: You'll own product strategy for Google's eWallet experiences — defining how users store, manage, and spend digital money. Day-to-day involves user research, feature definition, working with payments infrastructure teams, and driving wallet adoption. Success means growing wallet usage, stored value, and transaction frequency.

title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
role_summary: PM for payments risk — building products that detect, prevent, and manage fraud and risk in Google's payments ecosystem, likely including Google Pay India.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB over-enforcement work at Meta is a near-perfect parallel: he identified that Meta's global enforcement model was creating false positives that devastated Indian SMBs, then influenced policy change and a new agentic resolution system. This is essentially payments risk thinking — balancing fraud prevention with legitimate user experience. His understanding of how Indian SMBs operate informally (which can look like fraud to global models) is a unique edge. 3 years PM experience minimum is well within range.
how_to_win: The SMB enforcement story IS the interview answer here. Frame it explicitly: 'I discovered that Meta's global enforcement model was over-penalising legitimate Indian businesses because it didn't account for how Indian SMBs actually operate. I built the case for a new resolution system that impacted 10M+ businesses and drove 2.3% India revenue uplift.' This directly maps to payments risk — building models that distinguish legitimate Indian payment behaviour from actual fraud. Emphasise your data analysis skills and field observation methodology.
job_description: You'll own risk products within Google's payments ecosystem — defining rules, models, and user experiences that balance fraud prevention with legitimate user access. Day-to-day involves analysing risk patterns, working with ML and engineering teams, and shipping features that reduce fraud while minimising false positives. Success means lower fraud rates without degrading user experience.

title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
role_summary: PM for Google's payments platform infrastructure — the underlying systems that power Google Pay and other payment experiences across Google products.
why_good_fit: While more platform/infra-oriented than pure consumer, this role still benefits from Jay's India payments ecosystem understanding. His SMB work at Meta shows he can think about platform-level problems (enforcement systems, resolution workflows) that affect millions of users. His experience with WhatsApp API and Paid Messaging at Meta demonstrates platform-level product thinking for business tools. 5 years PM experience required matches well.
how_to_win: Frame your Meta experience as platform thinking: the SMB enforcement fix wasn't just a feature — it was a platform-level change to how Meta's systems handled Indian business accounts. Reference your understanding of India's payments infrastructure (UPI, NPCI) and how platform decisions affect downstream consumer and merchant experiences. Show you can balance technical platform requirements with market-specific needs.
job_description: You'll own parts of Google's payments platform — the infrastructure layer that enables payment experiences across Google products. Day-to-day involves defining platform capabilities, working with engineering teams, and ensuring the platform supports diverse payment methods and markets. Success means platform reliability, scalability, and enabling new payment experiences.

title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play
role_summary: PM for AI-powered gaming experiences on Google Play — likely building AI features that enhance game discovery, engagement, or developer tools within the Play Store gaming vertical.
why_good_fit: This role sits at the intersection of AI product and consumer platform — two of Jay's strengths. His AI fluency (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS, 20+ AI user interviews) combined with his consumer product experience (Resso, YouTube-adjacent work) makes him a strong candidate. His understanding of Indian gaming consumers (mobile-first, Tier 2/3 growth) is valuable for Google Play's India gaming strategy. The 5-year PM requirement and AI/GenAI domain experience align with his profile.
how_to_win: Lead with your AI product intuition — the MetaAI field work insight that 'language as a product growth lever' matters more than factual correctness for Indian users. Apply this to gaming: AI features in games need to feel natural and engaging for Indian users, not just technically impressive. Reference your Resso experience to show you understand entertainment product engagement loops. Demonstrate your AI OS building as evidence of hands-on AI product thinking.
job_description: You'll define and ship AI-powered features for the gaming vertical on Google Play — potentially AI-driven game recommendations, developer tools, or in-game AI experiences. Day-to-day involves working with AI/ML teams, game developers, and Play Store product teams. Success means increased gaming engagement and developer adoption of AI tools on Play.

title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
role_summary: PM for Google Play's apps ecosystem — managing product experiences around app discovery, distribution, and developer-facing features on the Play Store.
why_good_fit: Jay's experience scaling Resso on the Play Store (from alpha to India's largest music streaming app) gives him firsthand understanding of the app ecosystem from the developer/publisher side. His understanding of Indian app consumption patterns, Tier 2/3 user behaviour, and regional content discovery is directly relevant. His competitive intelligence work and market growth experience at Meta/ByteDance translate well to platform ecosystem thinking. 5 years PM experience matches.
how_to_win: Flip the perspective: 'I've been on the other side of Google Play — I scaled Resso from alpha to India's #1 music streaming app through the Play Store. I understand what developers need from the platform and where the gaps are for Indian app discovery.' Reference your understanding of how Indian users discover and adopt apps differently from global patterns. Show your data-driven approach (10%/90% content insight) applied to app store optimisation.
job_description: You'll own product features within Google Play's apps vertical — potentially app discovery, recommendations, developer tools, or monetisation features. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy, running experiments, working with engineering, and engaging with the developer ecosystem. Success means growing app installs, developer satisfaction, and Play Store engagement.

title: Product Manager, Applied AI, Google Cloud
role_summary: PM for Applied AI products within Google Cloud — building GenAI/LLM-powered products and features for enterprise and developer customers on Google Cloud Platform.
why_good_fit: This role is AI-native (Jay's non-negotiable) and requires GenAI/LLM domain experience — Jay has this through his top 5% Metamate usage, personal AI OS with multiple agents, and 20+ AI user interviews covering enterprises, SMBs, and consumers. His insight that 'language is a product growth lever' for Indian AI users demonstrates product intuition for LLM products. The 5-year PM requirement with 2 years 0-to-1 matches his Resso founding team and startup experience. B2B/Cloud is a medium fit but the AI-native component elevates it.
how_to_win: Lead with your AI fluency credentials: personal AI OS built on Metamate (multiple agents, 80% task automation, top 5 percentile user), 20+ AI user interviews across segments, and the 'language as product growth lever' insight. Frame your understanding of how Indian enterprises and SMBs adopt AI differently from global assumptions — this is your unique edge for Cloud AI products targeting the India market. Reference your field work methodology as the approach you'd bring to understanding enterprise AI adoption.
job_description: You'll own Applied AI product features on Google Cloud — defining how enterprises use GenAI/LLM capabilities through Cloud APIs, tools, and platforms. Day-to-day involves working with AI/ML research teams, enterprise customers, and Cloud engineering to ship AI products. Success means enterprise adoption of Google Cloud's AI offerings and revenue growth.

title: Senior Product Manager, Android Developer Console
role_summary: Senior PM for Android Developer Console — the dashboard and tools that Android app developers use to publish, manage, and grow their apps on the Play Store.
why_good_fit: Jay has developer-side experience from Resso (publishing and scaling an app through the Android ecosystem) and understands what developers need from platform tools. His SMB product thinking (understanding how smaller developers/businesses interact with complex platforms) is relevant. The 5-year PM requirement fits. However, this is a developer tools/B2B product without a strong India market growth or AI component, which limits the score.
how_to_win: Position your Resso experience as understanding the developer journey from the inside — what it's like to publish, iterate, and scale an app through Android. Reference your SMB enforcement work to show you understand how platform tools can inadvertently hurt smaller developers. Emphasise your zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team, startup) as evidence you can build developer-facing products from scratch.
job_description: You'll own features within the Android Developer Console — the tools developers use to publish apps, track performance, manage releases, and grow their user base. Day-to-day involves working with engineering, UXR, and the developer community to improve the console experience. Success means developer satisfaction, faster app publishing, and better developer tooling.

title: Product Manager, Google Cloud Business Platform
role_summary: PM for Google Cloud's business platform — likely internal business tools, CRM, or commerce platform capabilities within the Cloud ecosystem.
why_good_fit: Jay's experience with business products (Meta's advertising tools, WhatsApp API, Paid Messaging) and his understanding of how businesses adopt technology platforms gives him relevant context. The 5-year PM requirement with 2 years 0-to-1 matches. However, this is a B2B Cloud product without a clear India market growth or AI-native component, which limits alignment with Jay's non-negotiables.
how_to_win: Frame your Meta experience as understanding how businesses adopt and use platform tools — reference the SMB enforcement work as evidence you can identify platform-level issues affecting business customers. Show your understanding of Indian enterprise and SMB technology adoption patterns. Emphasise your cross-functional collaboration skills across product, engineering, and policy teams.
job_description: You'll own product features within Google Cloud's business platform — potentially commerce, CRM, or business operations tools for Cloud customers. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy, working with enterprise customers and engineering teams, and shipping platform capabilities. Success means enterprise adoption and platform revenue growth.

title: Product Manager, Cloud Database
role_summary: PM for Google Cloud's database products — managing cloud database services for enterprise and developer customers.
why_good_fit: Jay has 5 years PM experience matching the requirement, and his zero-to-one building experience is relevant. However, this is a deeply technical B2B infrastructure product requiring database domain expertise (2 years in databases/analytics/big data) that Jay doesn't have. No India market growth or AI-native component. Moderate fit at best.
how_to_win: If pursuing, emphasise your ability to translate complex technical products into user-friendly experiences (Resso's ML recommendation engine, Metamate AI OS). Frame your data analysis skills and AI-assisted analytics capability. This would be a stretch role — only pursue if other higher-fit roles don't materialise.
job_description: You'll own Google Cloud database products — defining features, working with database engineering teams, and serving enterprise customers who need managed database solutions. Day-to-day involves technical product strategy, customer engagement, and competitive positioning. Success means database product adoption and Cloud revenue.

title: Product Marketing Manager, Google Search
role_summary: Product Marketing Manager for Google Search in India — driving marketing strategy, campaigns, and user growth for Search products in the Indian market.
why_good_fit: This is an India market growth role with a product lens — aligning with Jay's core strength. His understanding of Indian user behaviour across segments (GenZ, Tier 2/3, regional/vernacular users) is directly relevant for Search marketing in India. His competitive intelligence work and market insight methodology translate well to product marketing. The 6-year marketing experience requirement is a stretch since Jay's experience is in product management, not marketing — but his insight-writing, pitching, and GTM work at Meta are marketing-adjacent.
how_to_win: Position your IPGS role at Meta as product marketing in practice — you wrote insights, built pitch decks, influenced product teams, and drove market growth. Reference your understanding of how Indian users search differently (voice-led, regional language, mobile-first). Use the MetaAI field work insight about language preferences to show you understand Search behaviour in India. Acknowledge the marketing title difference but emphasise your GTM and market growth experience.
job_description: You'll own product marketing for Google Search in India — defining positioning, running campaigns, driving user awareness of new Search features, and growing Search usage. Day-to-day involves cross-functional work with product, comms, and agency teams. Success means Search user growth and feature adoption in India.

title: Product Marketing Manager, Google Play Core Growth
role_summary: Product Marketing Manager driving core growth for Google Play — likely focused on user acquisition, engagement, and developer ecosystem marketing.
why_good_fit: Growth-focused role on a consumer platform Jay knows well from the developer side (Resso on Play Store). His understanding of Indian app ecosystem, Tier 2/3 user acquisition, and competitive dynamics is relevant. Similar to the Search PMM role, the 6-year marketing requirement is a stretch but his IPGS work is marketing-adjacent. The 'Core Growth' focus aligns with his growth PM experience.
how_to_win: Lead with your Resso growth story — scaling from alpha to India's largest music streaming app, understanding what drives app discovery and adoption for Indian users. Reference your understanding of how Tier 2/3 Indian users discover apps differently. Frame your IPGS role as growth marketing in practice. Show knowledge of Play Store's competitive position vs. alternative app stores in India.
job_description: You'll drive growth marketing for Google Play — defining strategies to grow Play Store usage, app installs, and developer engagement. Day-to-day involves campaign planning, cross-functional collaboration, data analysis, and market research. Success means growing Play Store metrics in target markets.

title: Product Marketing Manager, Subscriptions and Consumer Growth Marketing
role_summary: Product Marketing Manager for Google's subscription products and consumer growth — driving marketing campaigns for products like YouTube Premium, Google One, etc. in India.
why_good_fit: Consumer growth marketing in India is Jay's territory. His experience scaling Resso (a subscription music product) in India gives him direct understanding of subscription product marketing challenges — pricing sensitivity, freemium conversion, regional user behaviour. His GenZ and Tier 2/3 user expertise is relevant for consumer growth campaigns. The 4-year marketing requirement is more achievable given his product marketing-adjacent work. ATL/BTL campaign experience is a gap.
how_to_win: Lead with Resso — you literally scaled a subscription music product in India and understand the Indian consumer's relationship with paid subscriptions (price sensitivity, family sharing, regional content as conversion driver). Reference your understanding of Indian GenZ willingness to pay for digital products. Acknowledge the ATL/BTL gap but emphasise your product-led growth approach and field research methodology.
job_description: You'll drive marketing for Google's subscription products in India — planning and executing integrated campaigns (ATL, digital, BTL) to grow subscriber bases. Day-to-day involves campaign strategy, agency management, cross-functional collaboration, and performance analysis. Success means subscription growth and consumer awareness.

title: mCommerce Marketing Manager, Apps Go-To-Market, Google Play
role_summary: Marketing Manager for mobile commerce apps on Google Play — driving GTM strategy for shopping/commerce apps within the Play Store ecosystem.
why_good_fit: Jay's Shoppertainment work at Meta (convergence of entertainment and commerce for Indian creators) and his understanding of Indian shopping behaviour give him relevant context. His SMB advertiser conversion work and understanding of how Indian businesses sell online is applicable. The mCommerce focus in India aligns with his market expertise. The 6-year marketing requirement is a stretch but his GTM and market growth work is relevant.
how_to_win: Lead with Shoppertainment — you helped define Meta India's biggest monetisation bet around the convergence of entertainment and commerce. Reference your understanding of how Indian consumers shop on mobile (social commerce, creator-led discovery, price comparison). Show knowledge of India's mCommerce landscape and how Play Store can better serve shopping app developers and users.
job_description: You'll drive GTM strategy for mobile commerce apps on Google Play — working with shopping app developers, running marketing programs, and growing the mCommerce vertical. Day-to-day involves developer engagement, campaign planning, and cross-functional collaboration. Success means growing mCommerce app installs and developer satisfaction on Play.

title: Strategy and Operations Lead, GBO Tools
role_summary: Strategy & Operations Lead for Google Business Operations tools — likely defining strategy and driving operational improvements for internal business tools and processes.
why_good_fit: Jay's 6 years of experience fits the requirement. His strategic thinking (TikTok re-entry strategy at CPO level, Shoppertainment big bet identification) and operational execution (half planning, insight-to-execution methodology) are relevant. His AI fluency could be a differentiator for tools strategy. However, this is an internal ops/tools role without a clear market growth or consumer product mandate, which limits alignment with Jay's strengths and preferences.
how_to_win: Frame your IPGS methodology as strategy and operations in practice — half planning, discovery, insight writing, pitching, execution tracking. Reference your AI OS (80% task automation) as evidence you can think strategically about tools and operational efficiency. Show your management consulting-style thinking through the TikTok re-entry strategy work.
job_description: You'll lead strategy and operations for Google's internal business operations tools — defining roadmaps, driving process improvements, and ensuring tools meet business needs. Day-to-day involves stakeholder management, strategic analysis, and program execution. Success means operational efficiency and tool adoption across Google's business operations.

title: Senior Associate, Go-to-Market Business Strategy and Operations
role_summary: Senior Associate in GTM Strategy & Operations — supporting go-to-market strategy, sales operations, and business planning for Google's commercial teams in India.
why_good_fit: Jay's 4+ years of experience meets the requirement. His GTM thinking (Shoppertainment, SMB advertiser conversion, competitive intelligence) and strategic analysis skills are relevant. The GTM focus has a market component. However, 'Senior Associate' may be a step down in seniority for Jay, and this is more sales operations than product strategy. The analytical and market intelligence components provide moderate fit.
how_to_win: Frame your Meta IPGS work as GTM strategy — you identified market opportunities, built business cases, and drove execution across product and non-product levers. Reference your competitive intelligence methodology and opportunity sizing skills. Show your understanding of Google's India advertising business and how GTM strategy drives revenue growth.
job_description: You'll support GTM strategy and operations for Google's India commercial teams — analysing market data, building business cases, optimising sales processes, and driving strategic initiatives. Day-to-day involves data analysis, stakeholder management, and strategic planning. Success means improved GTM efficiency and revenue growth.

title: Core GTM Product Specialist, Google Play
role_summary: GTM Product Specialist for Google Play — working with app developers and content businesses to drive adoption and growth on the Play Store platform.
why_good_fit: Jay's experience scaling Resso through the Play Store and his understanding of the Indian app/content ecosystem are relevant. His SMB and advertiser growth work at Meta translates to developer/publisher engagement. The 8-year experience requirement is a stretch for Jay's ~7 years, but the role says 'product management, product specialist, or similar role' which gives some flexibility. The e-commerce/gaming/digital content experience requirement partially aligns with his Shoppertainment and Resso work.
how_to_win: Lead with Resso — you were on the developer side of Google Play, scaling a content app from alpha to India's largest music streaming platform. Reference your understanding of what content businesses need from platform partners. Show your Shoppertainment and commerce expertise for the e-commerce angle. Be prepared to address the slight experience gap.
job_description: You'll work as a GTM product specialist for Google Play — engaging with app developers and content businesses, advising on Play Store best practices, and driving platform adoption. Day-to-day involves developer consulting, product expertise, and cross-functional collaboration. Success means developer satisfaction and Play Store ecosystem growth.

title: Go-to-Market Operations Lead, Google Play
role_summary: GTM Operations Lead for Google Play — managing operational processes, project management, and customer-facing activities for the Play Store's go-to-market efforts.
why_good_fit: Jay's project management and cross-functional execution skills from Meta (half planning, execution partnership, stakeholder management) are relevant. His Play Store experience from the Resso side gives context. The 8-year requirement in project management or customer-facing role is a stretch. This is more operations than product/strategy, which limits alignment with Jay's strengths.
how_to_win: Frame your IPGS methodology as operational excellence — structured half planning, milestone tracking, execution partnership across teams. Reference your Resso experience for Play Store context. Emphasise your AI-driven operational efficiency (80% task automation). Be prepared to address the experience gap.
job_description: You'll lead GTM operations for Google Play — managing processes, coordinating cross-functional teams, and ensuring smooth execution of go-to-market programs. Day-to-day involves project management, stakeholder coordination, and operational improvement. Success means efficient GTM execution and program delivery.
